detail:
WMI-3 attacker content in authoritative position (offset-controlled-write): external content is written at an attacker-controlled offset ('param_kernel') into object 'struct dm_ioctl' — reaching past its buffer into a trailing field (the authoritative position the consumer follows). \[mechanism:write-at-attacker-offset;indicates=WMI-3\] \[mem:struct=dm_ioctl;field=param_kernel;offset=?\] \[oracle:assert=param_kernel stays within the object's declared buffer;assume=external content written at a variable offset into the object\]
WMI-3 attacker content in authoritative position (offset-controlled-write): external content is written at an attacker-controlled offset ('computed_offset') into object 'struct dm_ioctl' — reaching past its buffer into a trailing field (the authoritative position the consumer follows). \[mechanism:write-at-attacker-offset;indicates=WMI-3\] \[mem:struct=dm_ioctl;field=computed_offset;offset=?\] \[oracle:assert=computed_offset stays within the object's declared buffer;assume=external content written at a variable offset into the object\]

WMI-3 attacker content into authoritative position (attacker-pointer-into-field): content is installed into struct field 'buffer_head.b_bdev' — the position the confused consumer later follows as a trusted reference. \[mechanism:attacker-pointer-into-field;indicates=WMI-3\] \[mem:struct=buffer_head;field=b_bdev;offset=48\] \[oracle:assert=b_bdev holds trusted, not attacker-determined, content;assume=externally-originated content installed into the field\]

WMI-3 Arbitrary Free: md_bitmap_free() traverses 'cluster_info' through a pointer that could be stale (type mddev is freed elsewhere). Elements reached through this traversal are freed here. If the pointer was reclaimed, attacker controls what gets freed.

WMI-3 Arbitrary Free: md_bitmap_free() traverses 'mddev' through a pointer that could be stale (type mddev is freed elsewhere). Elements reached through this traversal are freed here. If the pointer was reclaimed, attacker controls what gets freed.
